era: 1997-present. Hip-Hop collective. E=5 -- sustained multi-element cultural energy. Central District. PNW chapter of the Universal Zulu Nation. FIRST COLLECTIVE ENTRY in the S36 engine. Four elements: DJing, MCing, Breaking, Graffiti. DJ Riz (136) is a member.

location.formative: Central District community spaces. Events across Seattle. Connected to Universal Zulu Nation (Bronx). The organizational framework for Seattle's four-element hip-hop culture.
location.career: 206 Zulu. PNW chapter of Universal Zulu Nation. Active since ~1997. Jams, cyphers, workshops, battles, anniversaries. Cultural preservation through active practice. Not a label or venue -- a network.

members.name: 206 Zulu collective
members.note: PNW chapter of Universal Zulu Nation. Collective entry -- multiple practitioners. Four-element hip-hop culture. DJ Riz (degree 136) is a member. Community-based. Central District.

associated_musicians.name: DJ Riz
associated_musicians.note: Community turntablist. E=5. Individual practitioner documented at 136; collective documented at 142.

associated_musicians.name: DJ Sounds
associated_musicians.note: 1983 pioneer. Pre-Nasty-Nes. The four-element culture DJ Sounds practiced is what 206 Zulu preserves.

associated_musicians.name: Afrika Bambaataa
associated_musicians.note: Founded UZN in the Bronx, 1973. 206 Zulu is the PNW chapter.

associated_musicians.name: Vitamin D
associated_musicians.note: Conception Records. Cultural infrastructure parallel: Conception (138), Mass Line (140), 206 Zulu (142).

associated_musicians.name: Sabzi
associated_musicians.note: Mass Line Media collective. Different infrastructure model: label/collective vs. cultural organization.

theory_nodes.concept: First collective entry -- documenting an organization rather than an individual artist; infrastructure rather than output; the framework that connects individual practitioners to cultural tradition
examples: All previous 141 entries were individual artists or groups.
206 Zulu is a cultural organization, not a musical act.
Documenting infrastructure: what supports the artists.
DJ Riz (136) as individual; 206 Zulu (142) as collective framework.
theory_nodes.description: First collective entry. Organization as subject. Infrastructure documentation.

ancestors: DJ Riz (136) as individual 206 Zulu member.

theory_nodes.concept: Four-element hip-hop culture -- DJing, MCing, Breaking, Graffiti (+ Knowledge as fifth); hip-hop as holistic cultural framework beyond music
examples: DJing: turntablism, vinyl, skill-focused, community service.
MCing: lyricism, freestyle cyphers, battle rap, conscious rap.
Graffiti: visual culture, murals, exhibitions, aesthetic.
Knowledge: self and community, cultural education, fifth element.

theory_nodes.concept: Cultural infrastructure trio -- Conception Records (138), Mass Line Media (140), 206 Zulu (142); three different organizational models supporting Seattle hip-hop
examples: Conception Records (138): Independent label. Release platform.
Mass Line Media (140): Collective/label hybrid. Events + releases.
206 Zulu (142): Cultural organization. Four-element practice. No releases.
theory_nodes.description: Three infrastructure models. Label, collective, organization.

genealogy.relationship: 206 Zulu at 142 completes the infrastructure trio.

cross_references.relationship: 206 ZULU MEMBER! DJ Riz (136, E=5, individual turntablist) is a member of 206 Zulu (142, E=5, collective). Individual at 136; collective at 142.
PREVIOUS DEGREE! DJ Sounds (141, E=5, 1983 pioneer) practiced the four-element culture that 206 Zulu preserves. Pioneer to preservation.
INFRASTRUCTURE PARALLEL! Mass Line Media (140, collective/label) and 206 Zulu (142, cultural organization). Different models, same community.
INFRASTRUCTURE TRIO! Conception Records (138), Mass Line Media (140), 206 Zulu (142). Three organizational models for Seattle hip-hop.
